name: Oportun
description_short: Fresno, CA's Oportun location on E Butler Ave offers personal loans with flexible terms for local residents seeking immediate lending solutions.
description_long:
Oportun's Fresno branch is located at 4817 E Butler Ave, a standalone storefront in central Fresno, CA. The location is open Monday through Friday from 11 AM to 6:30 PM, Saturday from 10 AM to 4 PM, and closed on Sundays. This street-level location makes it accessible for Fresno residents seeking personal loan services during extended weekday hours.

At this Fresno location, customers can explore personal loan options designed to meet various financial needs. The staff are trained to discuss loan terms, eligibility requirements, and the application process. Visit 4817 E Butler Ave in Fresno, CA to speak directly with a loan officer about flexible lending solutions tailored to your situation.

If you're a Fresno, CA resident considering a personal loan, bring a valid ID and proof of income to your appointment. This Oportun branch serves residents throughout central California who need transparent lending with manageable repayment terms.
pros: Designed for thin-file and no-credit borrowers typically rejected by banks
Offers both unsecured personal loans and secured (auto-title) loans
Reports to major credit bureaus — helps borrowers build credit history
Cash payments accepted at hundreds of retail locations (grocery stores, pharmacies, corner stores)
Fully bilingual service in English and Spanish
Nationwide branch network with in-person application support
Does not require a traditional credit score to apply
cons: APRs are substantially higher than prime personal loan lenders
Secured loan option puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession
PayNearMe cash payment carries an additional fee ($1.75 cash / $1.49 debit)
Limited value for borrowers who already have established credit and can qualify for better rates
In-person document requirements (car title, vehicle photos) add friction for secured loan applicants
best_for: Borrowers with no credit history or a thin credit file
Unbanked or underbanked consumers who pay bills in cash
Spanish-speaking applicants seeking bilingual loan support
Consumers looking to establish a credit history through installment loan payments
diagnosis: Oportun is best suited for credit-invisible consumers — particularly unbanked or immigrant borrowers — who need an installment loan and a path to building credit with minimal documentation barriers. The main caveat is cost: APRs are high relative to mainstream lenders, and borrowers with any established credit history should exhaust prime options first.
services: In-person branch application with document review
Cash loan payments via retail network (grocery stores, pharmacies, corner stores)
Credit bureau reporting for payment history
meta_description: Fresno, CA's Oportun at 4817 E Butler Ave. Personal loans with flexible terms, transparent rates, and quick approval.
